title: Cloezia × glaberrima
taxonomy: Cloezia × glaberrima is a hybrid of the genera Cloezia and Pentanisia, but it is most closely related to the species Pentanisia prittwitzii. The genus Pentanisia is classified under the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. Within the family Rubiaceae, the genus Pentanisia is part of the subfamily Ixoroideae. The subfamily Ixoroideae is further divided into several tribes, with Pentanisia being part of the tribe Vanguerieae. Cloezia × glaberrima's classification reflects its unique evolutionary history and its relationship to other species within the Rubiaceae family.

properties: Cloezia × glaberrima has been traditionally used in various folk remedies to treat ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its leaves and stems are believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and reduce swelling. Additionally, the plant is thought to have antimicrobial properties, making it potentially useful in treating infections and wounds. However, more research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Cloezia × glaberrima. Its leaves are also used to make a tea that is said to have antiseptic and antiviral properties, among other uses.

intro: Cloezia × glaberrima, also known as Cloezia, is a hybrid plant species in the family Myrtaceae, native to Australia. The leaves and bark of Cloezia × glaberrima have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Cloezia × glaberrima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Cloezia × glaberrima is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
